RULING:

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted as amended: Defendant Cal-Steam Supply Co.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. RULING:
DEFENDANT CRANE CO.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Crane Co.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1 through 4 (negligence and strict liability) and off calendar as to issues 5 and 6 (false representation and punitive damages). Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to an asbestos-containing product or material attributable to defendant (the asbestos content issue). Defendant also failed to sustain its burden of production of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that any exposure to asbestos attributable to defendant was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to his asbestos-related disease. Plaintiff's deposition testimony and responses to defendant's written contention discovery preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. The court did not consider the evidence submitted with defendant's reply. Plaintiff represented that he has dismissed his false representation cause of action and claim for punitive damages. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported = (503/TLJ)
